subject: BEU rep meeting agenda - Dec 10
snippet: Hi David, attaching the agenda for tomorrow's building rep meeting. Main item is contract campaign update and rally logistics for the 17th.
body:
Hi David,

Attaching the agenda for tomorrow's building rep meeting. Main items:
1. Contract campaign update - citywide petition numbers
2. Rally logistics for Dec 17 at City Hall Plaza
3. Grievance status update - hall duty case
4. New member sign-ups this spring

See you at 3pm in Room 214.

Kevin

subject: Re: BEU rep meeting agenda - Dec 10
snippet: Thanks Kevin, I'll have the Eastbridge petition count for you. We're at 47 signatures out of 62 eligible members. Room 214 works.
body:
Thanks Kevin,

I'll have the Eastbridge petition count ready for you. We're at 47 signatures out of 62 eligible members, up 8 since last week.

Room 214 works. I'll bring the sign-in sheet.

David

subject: Etsy: New review on APUSH DBQ Packet - Unit 5
snippet: You received a 5-star review: 'This packet is exactly what my students needed for the labor unit. The documents are well-selected and the scaffolding is perfect.'
body:
Hi APUSHResourceVault,

You have a new 5-star review on your listing:

APUSH DBQ Packet - Unit 5 Industrialization and Labor

Review: 'This packet is exactly what my students needed for the labor unit. The documents are well-selected and the scaffolding is perfect. Will be purchasing more packs from this shop!'

Reviewer: apushteacher.boston

Your Etsy team

subject: Jacksonville flights for December
snippet: Hey, I found a Spirit flight December 22 at 7am. Gets us into JAX by 10:30. Want me to book it? Frank said they'll pick us up.
body:
Hey,

Found a Spirit flight December 22 at 7am out of Logan. Gets us into JAX by 10:30. Want me to book it?

Frank said they'll pick us up from the airport. Mom is really excited to see us.

Also, do you want to try that Haitian place on Adams St. tomorrow night? I've been wanting to go for weeks.

Rachel

subject: BPS: Grade submission deadline reminder - June 20
snippet: Reminder: Final grade submissions for SY2025-26 are due by 11:59pm on June 20, 2026. Please log in to Aspen to submit.
body:
Dear Eastbridge High School Faculty,

This is a reminder that final grade submissions for School Year 2025-26 are due by 11:59pm on Friday, June 20, 2026.

Please log in to the Aspen Student Information System at aspen.bps.edu to submit grades.

Contact your department chair if you have questions about grade calculations for AP courses.

Boston Public Schools
Office of Academic Affairs

subject: APUSH DBQ generator - Period 4 duplicate issue
snippet: David, when I requested 8 Period 4 prompts today it repeated the Jacksonian Democracy one twice. Is this a known bug?
body:
David,

When I requested 8 Period 4 prompts from your DBQ generator today, it repeated the Jacksonian Democracy prompt twice after the 6th one.

Is this a known bug? My students noticed immediately and it was a bit awkward. Happy to test a fix whenever you have one.

Teresa

subject: Sunday dinner this week?
snippet: Hey Frank, you and Linda doing Sunday dinner? Rachel and I are in. I can bring the pasta if Mom's doing the sauce.
body:
Hey Frank,

You and Linda doing Sunday dinner this week? Rachel and I are in.

I can bring the pasta if Mom's doing the sauce. Rachel mentioned she found that good focaccia from Fornax. She'll grab a loaf.

Let us know what time.

David

subject: Dec 17 Rally - Building Rep Mobilization Guide
snippet: Fellow building reps, attached is the mobilization guide for the Dec 17 rally at City Hall Plaza. Your goal is 5 confirmed attendees per chapter.
body:
Fellow BEU Building Representatives,

Attached is the mobilization guide for the Dec 17 Contract Campaign Rally at City Hall Plaza, 4:30pm.

Your goal: 5 confirmed attendees from your building.

Talking points:
- 5% annual raise for 3 years
- 30-minute protected prep period daily
- Class size cap of 28 students

Please send your RSVP list to kmonteiro-beu@beuboston.org by Dec 14.

In solidarity,
BEU Organizing Department

subject: Hall duty grievance - Article 20 filing
snippet: Patricia, Kevin and I are filing a formal grievance under Article 20 regarding the hall duty assignment policy at Eastbridge. Attaching the documentation.
body:
Patricia,

Kevin and I are filing a formal grievance under Article 20 (Working Conditions and Hours) regarding the unilateral changes to hall duty assignments at Eastbridge High School.

The policy change was implemented without the required consultation with BEU per the contract. We have documented the dates and affected members.

Please find attached:
- Formal grievance form
- Contract language excerpt (Article 20, Section 3)
- List of affected members (8 Eastbridge teachers)

We look forward to hearing from the district within the contractual 10-business-day window.

In solidarity,
David Russo, BEU Eastbridge Building Rep

subject: MOHELA: Your PSLF payment count update
snippet: Your Public Service Loan Forgiveness qualifying payment count has been updated. You now have 87 qualifying payments on record.
body:
Dear David Russo,

Your Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) qualifying payment count has been updated.

Current qualifying payment count: 87 of 120 required
Expected forgiveness date: March 2028 (estimated)
Loan servicer: MOHELA

You are on track for full forgiveness after 33 more qualifying monthly payments.

Please log in to studentaid.gov to verify your employment certification is current.

MOHELA Customer Service

subject: APUSH Timeline Tool - Safari issue
snippet: David, the timeline tool is completely blank for my students on Safari 16 MacBooks. Chrome works. Console shows a CSS error about subgrid.
body:
David,

The timeline tool is completely blank for my AP students on the lab MacBooks running Safari 16. Chrome works fine on the same machines.

The console error is: 'CSS grid subgrid is not supported in this browser.'

Can you share a screenshot of what it should look like so I can at least show them the layout? And let me know when the Safari fix is coming. We have a review session next week.

James

subject: Labor-Management Committee meeting - June 25
snippet: David and Kevin, the June LMC meeting is confirmed for June 25 at 9am at BPS central office. Agenda includes the Eastbridge hall duty grievance and substitute coverage policy.
body:
David and Kevin,

The June Labor-Management Committee meeting is confirmed for:

Date: June 25, 2026
Time: 9:00am
Location: BPS Central Office, 2300 Washington St, Roxbury - Room 312

Agenda items:
1. Eastbridge hall duty grievance - Article 20
2. District-wide substitute coverage policy changes
3. Professional development budget for SY2026-27

Please come prepared with documentation for the hall duty case.

Nathan Ford
BEU-BPS Labor Relations Coordinator

subject: Re: History Club Gettysburg wrap-up
snippet: Teresa, thanks for helping chaperone. The students were incredible. Bowdoin monument was the highlight. Photos are in the shared Drive folder.
body:
Teresa,

Thank you again for helping chaperone the Gettysburg trip. The students were incredible. The way they engaged with the Bowdoin monument was exactly what I hoped for.

All photos are in the shared Google Drive folder (link in the History Club channel). I'm going to assign a reflection piece for the last week of school based on the trip.

Will you write a short paragraph about the monument for the History Club newsletter? Students would love a teacher reflection.

David

subject: Mom's birthday dinner - Dec 12
snippet: David, Mom wants dinner at Lucia's on the North End for her birthday. Saturday December 12, 6pm. Frank, Linda, Scott, and us. Can you and Rachel make it?
body:
David,

Mom wants to do dinner at Lucia's on the North End for her birthday. She specifically requested it.

Saturday December 12, 6pm reservation.
Frank, Linda, Scott, you and Rachel. Everyone should be there.

Can you two make it? Let me know by Sunday so I can finalize the reservation.

Marie

subject: Etsy Seller: You have a new message from a buyer
snippet: A buyer has sent you a message about 'APUSH Period 7 Complete Lesson Bundle'. They are asking if the LEQ prompts are aligned to the 2025 College Board framework.
body:
Hi APUSHResourceVault,

You have a new message from buyer historyteach22:

'Hi! I love your Period 5 and 6 packs. Quick question before I buy the Period 7 bundle. Are the LEQ prompts aligned to the updated 2025 College Board course framework? I know they revised the thematic learning objectives. Thanks!'

Reply to this message from your Etsy shop: etsy.com/your/messages

Etsy Support Team

subject: Parent conference sign-up sheet - Period 3 and 5
snippet: Angela, I'm sharing the parent conference sign-up sheet for my Period 3 and 5 APUSH classes. Can you review the time slots before I send to parents?
body:
Angela,

I'm sharing the parent conference sign-up sheet for my Period 3 and 5 APUSH sections. The grid is in the shared Drive folder.

Time slots are 3:30-6:00pm on November 18. Can you take a quick look at the format before I send the link to parents on Friday?

Also, I have two students whose parents I really want to connect with this round. I'll flag them in the sheet.

David

subject: NEA Labor History in the Classroom - Summer Institute 2026
snippet: Dear David, your application for the 2026 NEA Labor History in the Classroom summer institute has been received. The institute runs June 24-26 in Washington DC.
body:
Dear David Russo,

Thank you for applying to the 2026 NEA Labor History in the Classroom Summer Institute.

Details:
Dates: June 24-26, 2026
Location: NEA Headquarters, Washington DC
Focus: Primary source-based labor history pedagogy, connecting labor history to current worker organizing

Your application is under review. You will receive a decision by June 15.

Flight and hotel stipends are available for confirmed participants.

Labor History Educators Network

subject: Christmas plans and Jacksonville trip
snippet: Dave, Mom and Dad are expecting everyone for at least a few days before Christmas. Linda and I will be there the 21st through 29th. You and Rachel flying down?
body:
Dave,

Mom and Dad are expecting everyone for at least a few days before Christmas. Linda and I will be there the 21st through the 29th.

Are you and Rachel flying down? Dad said to book early. Flights to JAX around Christmas fill up fast.

Marie said she is driving up from Providence. Should be a full house.

Frank

subject: Primary source API rate limit - school IP question
snippet: Derek, I have a classroom API tool that some BPS teachers have started using. When a whole class hits it from school WiFi, they're getting rate limit errors. I think it's because we all share one egress IP.
body:
Derek,

I built a simple primary source API that a few APUSH teachers across BPS have started using in class. The issue: when a whole class (28 students) uses it simultaneously from school WiFi, they hit rate limit errors after about 10 seconds.

I'm guessing this is because all traffic from school WiFi goes through a single egress IP, and my per-IP limit (20 req/min) is too low for classroom use.

Can you confirm how BPS school WiFi handles egress IPs? That would help me decide whether to raise the IP limit or move to per-session tokens.

David

subject: Celtics Game 5 - Thursday 7:30
snippet: David, Celtics Game 5 is Thursday at 7:30. Me and a couple guys from the Southie crew. TD Garden seats in section 312. The Ashmont Tap after? You in?
body:
David,

Celtics Game 5 is Thursday at 7:30 at TD Garden. Me and a couple of guys from the Southie crew.

Seats are in section 312, should be a great view.

The Ashmont Tap after if they win? Or before if you want to grab food first.

You in?

Kevin

subject: Sunday dinner this week
snippet: David, are you and Rachel coming Sunday? Frank is making the sauce. Marie said she might drive up. Let me know by Friday so I know how much pasta to buy.
body:
David,

Are you and Rachel coming Sunday? Frank is making the sauce and the meatballs.

Marie said she might drive up from Providence if she can finish her case notes Friday. Scotty said he is in.

Let me know by Friday so I know how much pasta to buy. And tell Rachel she does not need to bring anything but I know she will anyway.

Love,
Mom

subject: Apprenticeship hours and Celtics tickets
snippet: Scotty, how are the apprenticeship hours stacking up? Kevin has a spare Celtics ticket for Game 5 if you want it. Thursday 7:30. Section 312.
body:
Scotty,

How are the apprenticeship hours stacking up? Dad said you logged your 4000th last month, that is a big one.

Kevin has a spare Celtics ticket for Game 5 if you want it. Thursday 7:30, section 312. Ashmont Tap after if they win.

Let me know.

David

subject: AP USH Labor Unit - draft feedback
snippet: David, read through your AP USH labor unit draft. The 1870 to 1970 arc is strong. A few notes on the Triangle Shirtwaist section and the Reagan-era ending.
body:
David,

Read through your AP USH labor unit draft over the weekend. The 1870 to 1970 arc is strong and the primary source selection is the best work I have seen from anyone in the department.

A few notes:

1. The Triangle Shirtwaist section could use one more contemporary newspaper account. I have a Forward clipping from 1911 you can use.

2. The Reagan-era ending feels rushed. Consider extending it by a class period so students see the PATCO strike in full context.

3. Make sure the LEQ aligns with the 2025 College Board framework for Period 8.

Let me know when you want to walk through revisions. I can do Thursday after school.

Patricia

subject: BEU leadership track conversation
snippet: David, want to grab coffee next week? I want to talk to you about the leadership track on the executive board. The position-cut fight has made it obvious you are ready.
body:
David,

Want to grab coffee next week? Wednesday morning before school works for me, or Saturday at Donut Depot if that is better for Rachel.

I want to talk to you about the leadership track on the executive board. The way you handled the hall duty grievance and the work you are doing on the position-cut fight has made it obvious you are ready.

I know you have been hesitant about the time commitment with grading and Grandma Carol. But the contract fight in February is going to need building reps who can move fast, and that is you.

No pressure. Just want to start the conversation.

Tom

subject: Grandma update - A1C and walker
snippet: David, took Grandma to her endocrinologist Tuesday. A1C is down to 7.4 from 7.8, which is the best she has been in two years. Walker is helping with the porch steps.
body:
David,

Took Grandma to her endocrinologist on Tuesday. A1C is down to 7.4 from 7.8, which is the best she has been in two years. Dr. Hadley said the Metformin dose is staying the same and they will check again in three months.

The walker is helping a lot with the porch steps. She had two good walks to the mailbox last week.

She asked about your Christmas trip dates. I told her you and Rachel are flying in the 22nd and staying through the 29th. She is already planning the menu.

Keep doing the Sunday calls, she lights up after every one.

Love,
Louise
